rulings.Case Title: MARY RAGO VS. WOOD STREET PLAZA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Yosemite Defendant?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Documents Subpoena Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions And Contempt Sanctions Against James Schubert
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 4, 2021, Line 1, DEFENDANTS SAMSON LAI, SUSAN LAI, AND YOSEMITE CAPITAL, LLC's Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Documents Subpoena Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions And Contempt Sanctions Against James Schubert. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel Third Party to Attend Deposition and for Sanctions: Although no opposition was filed by either plaintiff or third party deponent, James Schubert, failure to comply with a subpoena subjects the non-complying party with contempt. (Code of Civ. Proc. Sec. 1991.1.) It appears that when a nonparty fails to obey a deposition subpoena. the court's power is limited to contempt proceedings as it has no jurisdiction over the nonparty. (Lund v. Sup. Ct. (1964) 61 Cal.2d 698, 713.) Therefore the motion is denied without prejudice to Defendants seeking appropriate relief. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARILYN A. BARTHOLOMEW VS. GOLDEN GATE TOW, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Daniel Stone To Form Interrogatories, Sets One, Two And Three
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 4, 2021, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MARILYN BARTHOLOMEW's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Daniel Stone To Form Interrogatories, Sets One, Two And Three (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling). Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories: Granted as to interrogatories 12.1, 12.6, 20.1, 20.9, 20.10. Plaintiff is entitled to know what sources you consulted and what you found even if they are mutually available. (Greyhound Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (1961) 56 Cal.2d 355, 386.). Granted as to interrogatory 15.1 in so far as a separate response should be made to each defense. With respect to interrogatory 17.1, its appears that the verification issue has been resolved with respect to past responses. It is noted at the outset that the request for admission are not very well drafted; similarly the responses are not well drafted or complete. As for the specific responses, #1 regarding the photo, the response is adequate and is it difficult to prove a negative and therefore the motion to compel is denied. #4, the request is compound and vague as to what is meant by "no person acting on behalf of your position of Property Manager" but defendant admits that Wes Jennings saw it but he was not acting on behalf of defendant, therefore the motion to compel is denied. #5 and #6, the requests assumes that Admiral Security is an agent of John Stewart. Defendant to respond whether Admiral Security is an agent of defendant and if so then further responses are required as defendant has the ability to obtain this information. #8 with respect to subdivision (b) the response is ambiguous either defendant does not know or no one contacted the DMV, therefore further responses required #14, 15, 18, 21 and 22 are confusing because in 21(d) and 22(e) there is reference to a written authorization yet 14, 15 and 18 do not refer to one. To the extent that defendant does not believe a written authorization was required, responses should be amended to reflect that one was not made. Therefore, further responses are required (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHILOH WADE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses And To Strike Defendant City And County Of San Francisco' S Objections To Plaintiff'S Request For Interrogatories-Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 4, 2021, Line 3, PLAINTIFF's Motion To Compel Further Responses And To Strike Defendant City And County Of San Francisco' S Objections To Plaintiff'S Request For Interrogatories-Set One (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of tentative ruling). Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel Further Responses to Interrogatories: CCSF responses were not received until May 3 and it is unclear whether Plaintiff filed a reply. Request for further responses form interrogatory 1.1 is granted regarding contact information for the various individuals subject to a protective order that this contact information is for attorneys eyes only and is not to be disclosed. Special interrogatories 5 and 15, objections are overruled and CCSF is to provide contact information for attorneys eyes only. With respect of form interrogatory 17.1, the accident occurred almost 3 years ago, and the complaint was filed approximately two years ago. Moreover, requests for admissions go to legal contentions. The objections are therefore overruled as to 1-7 are overruled and CCSF is to respond based on its current knowledge from whatever sources independent of plaintiff's deposition. With regard to requests, 8, 9, 10 and 11, CCSF needs to disclose the contact information for attorney eyes only. Moreover, while the CCSF may not maintain cameras, requests #8 and 9 are not so limited. Therefore further responses are required. With regard to 10 and 11, CCSF states it did not get any letter and in so far as it does not do video recording at the site, there is nothing to preserve unless it obtained video from a third party. (Tentative Ruling Continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELOY ROBLES VS. JACK STEPHENSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One, Form Interrogatories (17.1), Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production, Set One Responses From Defendant Jack Stephenson And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, May 4, 2021, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ELOY ROBLES' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One, Form Interrogatories (17.1), Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Production, Set One Responses From Defendant Jack Stephenson And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further to Various Discovery: Parties to appear. Plaintiff relying on the case of John B. v. Sup. Ct. (2006) 38 Cal.4th 1177, claims he is entitled to broad ranging discovery of private information as the result of his allegation that he acquired HPV, a sexually transmitted disease, from defendant. John B. does not appear to allow for carte blanche discovery for each of the discovery items at issue rather each discovery item must be addressed separately. (Id. at 1199; compare Id. at 1207 (Kennard, J. concurring and dissenting "Here, the majority's limitations on discovery are not necessary.") Parties to meet and confer prior to the hearing to determine if particular discovery is "narrowly drawn" and whether particular discovery requests can be eliminated on that basis. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR VS. HEALTHRIGHT 360 ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Demand For Production, Set One, And Imposing Monetary Sanction; Declaration; And, Memorandum.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 4, 2021, Line 1, PLAINTIFF RAYMOND DIGIACOMO'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, And Demand For Production, Set One, And Imposing Monetary Sanction. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion for an order compelling further responses to form interrogatories is granted as to interrogatories 15.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.4, 50.5 and 50.6 within 15 days of the issuance of an order. The parties are ordered to engage in good faith efforts to meet and confer regarding defendants' production of documents before filing a subsequent motion to compel production. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $3.00 to plaintiff, payable within 10 days of issuance of the order. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN J. KAUFMAN ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA FRANCISE TAX BOARD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, May 4, 2021, Line 6, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA FRANCISE TAX BOARD'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The court declines Plaintiffs' request to take judicial notice of the legislative history. The language of the statutes is not ambiguous. "[T]he objective of statutory interpretation is to ascertain and effectuate legislative intent. In determining intent, we look first to the words themselves. When the language is clear and unambiguous, there is no need for construction." (Freeman v. Wal-Mart Stores, Inc. (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 660, 665.) (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to all counsel) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
